description_long: This is a mid-rise Soviet-era apartment building with a flat, dark roof and a rectangular footprint. The facade is clad in light gray concrete panels, featuring rows of uniformly spaced windows, some of which have white or brown frames. Several balconies with glass or metal railings are visible, particularly on one side, and a green main entrance door is present at the base. The building has five stories, each with a repetitive window arrangement, and a utilitarian, functional design typical of Eastern European residential blocks. The exterior shows signs of weathering and age, with some windows open or covered by curtains. The overall appearance is stark and practical, emphasizing function over ornamentation.
description: A five-story, gray concrete apartment building with uniform windows, balconies, and a flat roof, typical of Soviet-era residential architecture.
description_view_0: The facade shows a grid of windows, some with curtains, and a section of balconies on the right side.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the continuation of the window pattern and balconies, with less visible entrance detail.
description_view_2: The front view features the main green entrance door, a full row of windows, and several balconies on the right.
description_view_3: This rear or side view displays the repetitive window arrangement and balconies, with no visible entrance.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in a regular, grid-like pattern with consistent spacing and alignment across all floors, occasionally interrupted by balconies.
